summary: Exact monthly active and distinct-working-driver percentages require accounting for every driver, ride, and acceptance row; the fixed twelve-month spine adds only constant work, matching the linear input lower bound.

optimality.argument: Any Drivers row can change an active denominator, any Rides row can change the request month of an accepted ride, and any AcceptedRides row can change a distinct working-driver numerator. Exact results therefore require Omega(d+r+a) input work. A join/group scan plus twelve fixed cutoff counts meets O(d+r+a).

replacement_checks.evidence: Changing one row in any input relation can alter a required monthly percentage, so all d+r+a rows must be accounted for.
The query scans and groups the ride/acceptance join, evaluates twelve fixed driver cutoffs, and joins a constant-size month spine in O(d+r+a) total work.

Regression compares every authored fixture with an independent calendar oracle covering distinct-driver counting, month-end joins, zero denominators, rounding, rejected requests, and out-of-year rides.
